expectations.text: Detects #f5f5f5 as a hardcoded hex color value and flags it as BLOCKING severity
expectations.evidence: Detected #f5f5f5 but categorized as 'Color' — no BLOCKING severity used. Uses generic 'Category' column instead.

expectations.text: Detects rgb(51, 51, 51) as a hardcoded color value and flags it as BLOCKING severity
expectations.evidence: Detected rgb(51,51,51) but categorized as 'Color' — no BLOCKING severity.

expectations.text: Detects 8px border-radius as a hardcoded value and flags it as WARNING severity
expectations.evidence: Detected 8px but categorized as 'Radius' — no WARNING severity.

expectations.text: Detects 16px/24px padding as hardcoded spacing and flags it as SERIOUS severity
expectations.evidence: Detected 16px 24px but categorized as 'Spacing' — no SERIOUS severity.

expectations.text: Detects 14px font-size as hardcoded typography and flags it as SERIOUS severity
expectations.evidence: Detected 14px but categorized as 'Typography' — no SERIOUS severity.

expectations.text: Suggests specific token replacements for hardcoded values
expectations.evidence: No token replacement suggestions provided. Only lists values and categories.

expectations.text: Does NOT flag existing correct token usage like var(--color-border-primary) or var(--space-300) as violations
expectations.evidence: 'Correct Token Usages' section lists these as correct.

expectations.text: Output follows a structured audit report format with clear sections (not freeform prose)
expectations.evidence: Has structured sections: 'Hardcoded Values' table, 'Correct Token Usages', 'Key Risks'.

expectations.text: Correctly distinguishes color track violations as BLOCKING from structural track violations as WARNING or SERIOUS
expectations.evidence: No BLOCKING/SERIOUS/WARNING severity system used at all. All findings use generic 'Category' labels.

expectations.text: References the token naming convention when suggesting replacement tokens
expectations.evidence: No token naming conventions referenced. No replacement tokens suggested.

eval_feedback.overall: Without-skill correctly detects hardcoded values but completely lacks the severity framework and token replacement guidance that the skill provides.
